papers.institution: Mr. Masao Dohi was born in Hyogo Prefecture, Japan, in 1965. He received his B.S. in physics in 1988 from Kobe University. He joined IDEC Corporation in 1988, where he engaged in R&D on semiconductor devices. Since 1999 he conducted product development on industrial networking devices, RF-ID equipment, and functional safety products. Since 2010, he had led the International Standardization and Safety Solution Center, and then he has led the international Standardization Division in R&D Strategy HQ. His expertise ranges widely from, machinery safety, functional safety, explosion-proof technology, human machine interface, robotics, industrial automation, field networks, and to fine bubble technology. He possesses in-depth knowledge of IEC and ISO international standards, mainly in the fields of safety and industrial automation, and is belonging to various activities related to standardization. He was awarded the achievement award by the Japan Robot Association (JARA) in 2017 for his contribution to the standardization committee and the planning committee in JARA.
